LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BLOOMINGDALE'S, INC. AND ANDRE LAWRENCE'S MOTION FOR DISCOVERY IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. ALL OF THE QUESTIONS THAT PLAINTIFF SEYEDEH KALANTARI WAS INSTRUCTED NOT TO ANSWER AT HER DEPOSITION IMPLICATE HER PRIVACY RIGHTS. RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Bloomingdale's, Inc. and Andre Lawrence's motion for discovery is denied in its entirety. Plaintiffs Motion For Relief From Deemed Waiver Of Objections To Request For Production
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF SEYEDEH KALANTARI's Motion For Relief From Deemed Waiver Of Objections To Request For Production.
This motion concerns late responses to a second set of Requests for Production of Documents ("RPDs") that Defendant Bloomingdales, Inc. propounded on Plaintiff. It appears that Plaintiff's counsel misplaced the RPDs, and did not know of them until after the deadline for responding to the RPDs had passed. Plaintiff now seeks relief from the waiver of objections.

On the record presented, the Court intends to grant the motion. Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300 allows for relief from waiver when a party serves a response that is in substantial compliance with CCP sections 2030.210 - .240 and 2030.280; and where the failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. The cases discussing the general rules governing excusable neglect provide a liberal standard for relief with a view to deciding controversies on their merits. (CCP 473; Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 234.)

The chronology of events provided by Plaintiff supports a finding that the failure to provide timely responses was a mistake due to excusable neglect, and that Defendant has not been prejudiced by the delay in obtaining responses.
It appears that the RPDs at issue in this motion are also the subject of a motion to compel which is set to be heard on February 23, 2018. (See Defendant Bloomingdale's, Inc. Motion to Compel Further Responses To Its Requests for Production of Documents, Set Two, filed on 1/30/2018.) The merits of Plaintiff's objections to the RPDs can be determined at the hearing of that motion. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Timothy Nardell, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302/JPT)
